disjointness_note: Disjoint from every banked loangenus task AND deconflicted from the concurrent builder loan6, who claimed agent/documents/document_qa/ (retrieval.py + service.py) — this task touches NONE of document_qa. Banked-sibling avoidance: latent-doc-extractors used documents/extractors/cre_fields.py; latent-credit-normalize used documents/credit_pdf/{junk_filter,normalize}.py; latent-financial-tools used agent/analytics/services/*; latent-market-structure used services/cre_qualification/*; latent-phone-invites used integrations/cartesia/phone.py + services/smbcontacts/loan_types.py. This task touches ONLY extraction/table_text.py, report/builders.py and deal_summary/portfolio.py — no file, function, or defect shape overlaps a sibling or loan6. Avoided loan6's document_qa + cartesia/voice/call cluster and the used ingestion/ subtree.
difficulty_mix: 2 EASY (D2 grid col-index half-open bound; D3 chart-gate >=2 pinned by 5 siblings) + 3 MEDIUM (D1 zero-amount inclusion; D4 card-return cap; D5 occupancy-weight division-by-zero guard).
partial_run_note: Reversed 2 of 5 (D2 grid-index + D3 chart-gate); the other three f2p (D1 zero-amount, D4 card-cap, D5 div-by-zero) still FAILED -> required 12/15 -> reward 0.
base_defects_visible_suite: GREEN — identical failure set to clean HEAD. Clean loangenus-repo:v1 and the five-defect planted image BOTH report 7 failed / 166 passed / 24 skipped, and the 7 failing node IDs are byte-for-byte identical (pre-existing rot: test_inbound_calls x3, test_document_ingestion_readiness x2, test_document_report::TestReportSupport::test_credit_and_bank_excluded x1, test_ingestion_stale_recovery x1). The suite that drives table_text (test_document_layout_extraction.py) is GREEN in both; the suite that drives builders (test_document_report.py) has only its one pre-existing rot failure in both; and the suite that drives portfolio (test_bridge_broadway_extraction.py) is SKIPPED in both (its setUp skips unless Windows sample PDFs + pymupdf are present), so all five edge-only defects are invisible.

ladder.build_plant: image builds from loangenus-repo:v1; plant script asserts each of the 5 unique-substring edits applied exactly once
ladder.in_image_leak_check: git log len 1, git diff empty, 0 modified, all 5 planted lines present
ladder.import_check: the gold suite's imports (table_text, report.builders, deal_summary.portfolio, enums) succeed offline in-image (builders transitively loads settings/qdrant/beanie via report/__init__, which works with the baked JWT_SECRET_KEY and no network; table_text and portfolio are stdlib-only)
ladder.null_run: reward 0; all 5 f2p FAILED with per-test lines (no collection ERRORs); all 10 p2p PASSED
ladder.full_suite_leak: clean vs planted failure node-ID sets diff-identical (7 failed / 166 passed / 24 skipped both); zero new failures

env_note: No task-specific env required. The document math is deterministic and offline; the JWT_SECRET_KEY baked into loangenus-repo:v1 is sufficient for the report.builders import chain. table_text.py and portfolio.py import only the standard library (re/typing); builders.py adds pydantic (a base dep) via report.schemas. Tests run under --network none.

defects.edge_input: text whose (only/last) monetary amount is exactly 0, e.g. an 'Ending Balance | 0.00' row
defects.wrong_output: a $0.00 amount is discarded and last_amount_in_text returns None; surfaces one call-hop up as a missing closing_balance in extract_bank_statement_fields
defects.derivability: DERIVABLE, MEDIUM. $0.00 is a valid monetary figure (a zero balance), and the money regex [\d,]+(?:\.\d{2})? never captures a sign, so the parsed value is always >= 0 and the guard exists only to admit every parsed amount including zero. `> 0` drops a legitimate zero. The distance f2p drives the downstream consumer extract_bank_statement_fields rather than last_amount_in_text directly.
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: test_document_layout_extraction.py feeds amounts 5 and 135099; only an exactly-zero amount differs between `>= 0` and `> 0`.

defects.edge_input: an Azure-DI cell whose column_index equals column_count (one past the last valid column)
defects.wrong_output: the out-of-range cell is no longer skipped; grid[row_idx][column_count] raises IndexError, crashing table serialization
defects.derivability: DERIVABLE, EASY (adjacent construction pin). The grid is built as [["" for _ in range(column_count)] for _ in range(row_count)] five lines above, so valid column indices are 0..column_count-1; a valid index must be `< column_count`. `<= column_count` admits an index one past the end and indexes past the row. Standard half-open-range off-by-one, pinned by the immediately-visible grid construction.
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: test_document_layout_extraction.py uses cells at columns 0,1,2 with column_count=3 (all strictly inside); no cell sits at the width, so `<` and `<=` agree.

defects.edge_input: a tax report with exactly two chartable currency amounts (e.g. gross_profit + gross_income)
defects.wrong_output: the 'Key Tax Amounts' bar_chart section (id 'tax_amounts_chart') is omitted; a two-amount comparison chart no longer renders
defects.derivability: DERIVABLE, EASY (five sibling gates). A comparison chart needs at least two points; the module's five sibling chart gates (_build_pfs_report, _build_financial_statement_report, _build_rent_roll_report, _build_debt_schedule_report, _build_t12_report) all use `>= 2` for the same render decision, so `> 2` is a one-line inconsistency with all five. The correct inclusive `>= 2` is uniquely pinned by the siblings.
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: test_document_report.py drives _build_tax_report with only one chartable amount (gross_profit), where 1 point yields no chart under both `>= 2` and `> 2`; its one pre-existing failure (TestReportSupport::test_credit_and_bank_excluded) is unrelated rot.

defects.boundary_changed: if len(cards) >= limit  ->  if len(cards) > limit
defects.edge_input: more money-keyword numeric facts than the cap, e.g. six such facts for a debt-schedule report (limit 5)
defects.wrong_output: returns limit+1 summary cards (6) instead of at most limit (5); the card cap is breached by one
defects.derivability: DERIVABLE, MEDIUM. The parameter `limit` (default 4; 5 for the debt-schedule/budget builders) is the maximum number of cards to emit; the loop appends a card then breaks once `len(cards) >= limit`, capping at exactly `limit`. `> limit` admits one extra card, violating the cap. The off-by-one after an append-then-check is the reasoning step. Consumed by six builders (lease/debt/budget/loan/construction/generic), so any of their reports over-fills by one card.
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: _cards_from_numeric_facts is not driven by any running test; the cap only binds when more than `limit` money-keyword facts are present, and both `>=` and `>` agree until the count reaches `limit`.

defects.edge_input: a property_appraisal portfolio whose properties carry no physical_occupancy/building_sf, so the occupancy weight sums to exactly 0
defects.wrong_output: the guard admits a zero denominator and `occ_weighted / occ_weight` raises ZeroDivisionError, crashing the portfolio merge (a portfolio-level appraisal aggregation)
defects.derivability: DERIVABLE, MEDIUM (normalization-divisor guard). The very next line divides by occ_weight (`out["physical_occupancy"] = occ_weighted / occ_weight`), so the guard must exclude a zero denominator; `> 0` is the only safe boundary and `>= 0` admits occ_weight == 0 -> ZeroDivisionError. The correct side is uniquely pinned by the division on the following line (a classic guard-before-divide).
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: the only test that drives merge_portfolio_property (test_bridge_broadway_extraction.py) is SKIPPED in-image (its setUp requires Windows sample PDFs + pymupdf) and its properties carry full occupancy/sf anyway (occ_weight > 0), so `>` and `>=` agree there.

breadth_distance_interaction: Breadth: 5 f2p across 3 source files (extraction/table_text.py, report/builders.py, deal_summary/portfolio.py) and 5 functions (last_amount_in_text/extract_bank_statement_fields, build_table_grid, _build_tax_report, _cards_from_numeric_facts, merge_portfolio_property) covering 5 distinct boundary shapes (numeric zero/sign, half-open array index, minimum-cardinality gate, collection-return cap, division-by-zero divisor guard). An agent that fixes only one literal symptom leaves >=2 hidden f2p failing. Distance: D1's f2p (test_zero_closing_balance_is_recorded) drives the downstream consumer extract_bank_statement_fields, whose missing closing_balance originates one call-hop earlier in last_amount_in_text. Interaction is intentionally light (the five defects sit on independent paths); breadth + distance carry the difficulty per construction rule 5.
grep_twin_audit: The five planted lines are distinct shapes with no shared grep signature: `if value > 0`, `col_idx <= column_count`, `len(chart_points) > 2`, `len(cards) > limit`, `if occ_weight >= 0`. No single pattern locates all five, and each sits in a different function; the two `len(...) >` lines differ in variable, bound and file location (precedented: latent-financial-tools shipped three distinct len-comparisons).
fairness_audit: All 5 f2p and 10 p2p are pure-input (str/list/dict/scalar in, scalar/list/dict out) and use ZERO mocks (only a SimpleNamespace stand-in for Azure-DI cells with explicit row_index/column_index/content). Each f2p asserts behavior at the exact edge, and each edge has a uniquely-derivable correct side (zero-is-a-valid-amount + sign-free regex; half-open range from the grid construction; five sibling chart gates at >= 2; `limit`-as-maximum cap semantics; guard-before-divide on the next line). Each p2p pins a just-off-edge value that no correct fix changes (a nonzero balance and a positive amount; an in-bounds cell and the last valid column; one-amount and three-amount reports; exactly-at-cap and below-cap card counts; a weighted average with nonzero weight and a non-appraisal merge). Alternative-implementation audit: restoring each boundary by any means (revert, rewrite, explicit clamp/guard) yields identical outputs on all 15 tests. Names referenced by the hidden tests (the three module paths and their public functions last_amount_in_text, extract_bank_statement_fields, build_table_grid, build_report_from_facts, merge_portfolio_property, plus DocumentType) all exist at base and are either named in the instruction's file references or forced by existing import sites. Deterministic, offline, no secrets.
litmus_result: PASS. Given only the instruction (the three named document files under loangen-agent/agent/documents/, symptom = wrong values on exact boundaries / edge cases; no function names, boundary directions, values, or count), a strong dev cannot locate the five defect lines in <2 minutes: the surface spans table_text.py (185 lines), builders.py (672 lines) and portfolio.py (165 lines), every comparison is a plausible boundary, no failing test bisects (the graded suite enters only at grade time and the visible suite is byte-for-byte green vs clean HEAD, same 7 rot failures), and the changes are single tokens. Once found, two boundaries (D2 grid index, D3 chart gate) are cleanly pinned (adjacent construction / five siblings) and three (D1 zero-amount, D4 card cap, D5 divide guard) are medium — pinned by zero-validity + a sign-free regex, cap semantics, and the guard-before-divide on the following line respectively. Breadth (5 f2p across 3 files / 5 shapes) and distance (D1 surfaces one call-hop up via extract_bank_statement_fields) both hold.
